events.text: {"decision":"fail","risk":"high","work_stage":"in-progress","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":50,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.85,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["GFCI/AFCI device not providing required protection to downstream receptacles as wired for single-location only where protection of downstream outlets is required","Common shortcut: using LINE-only wiring and skipping LOAD-side connections leaves downstream receptacles unprotected in areas requiring GFCI/AFCI coverage"],"actions":["Verify whether downstream receptacles require protection per NEC 210.8/210.12","If downstream outlets are in required locations, rewire using LOAD terminals to extend protection or install individual GFCI/AFCI protection at each required receptacle","Test all protected receptacles with a tester to confirm trip function","Correct wiring before energizing downstream loads"],"rationale":"Wiring a GFCI/AFCI device for single-location protection only, when downstream receptacles in required locations are present and unprotected, is a non-compliant shortcut. NEC 210.8 and 210.12 require protection at all required receptacles; the LOAD terminals must be used to extend protection or each receptacle must be individually protected.","references":["NEC 210.8","NEC 210.12"]}
